response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core demands of this role. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month position at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis side project — neither demonstrates the deep STL, multi-threading, or systems-level C++ mastery required. His primary stack throughout his career is TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, SDL practices, or IoT protocol experience (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, etc.). The advantageous skills he does possess (React, Node.js) carry minimal weight against the absence of the role's core competencies.
strengths: Event-driven and message-broker architecture experience via the open-source GISCollective geo-data platform
High-throughput, low-latency concurrent programming experience at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang handling millions of requests/second)
React and Node.js proficiency aligns with the role's listed advantageous skills
concerns: C++ expertise is negligible — roughly 6 months over a decade ago and a thesis project; primary languages are TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js
Zero demonstrated knowledge of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity practices
No background in industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850), or embedded/on-premises platform development
